standard_live_change.description: Routine live mutation that may be requested by an agent or operator, but still requires human approval and evidence planning.

operator_approved.description: A promotion or controlled live change that may be requested by an agent or operator, but still requires an explicit human-operator approval before execution.

sensitive_live_change.description: Higher-risk host, provisioning, security, or boundary-changing mutation that must stay operator-initiated and human-approved.

Stop if an operator attempts to bypass the blocked preflight with ad hoc shell commands.
rollback_guidance: Do not use this path for live backup configuration in the current platform model.

rollback_guidance: Reapply the last known-good DNS state from the repo before retrying.

Stop if the Hetzner DNS update or edge re-apply would drift from the catalogled target or hostname set.
rollback_guidance: Reapply the last known-good repo state for the affected hostname before retrying.
Do not create ad hoc DNS or NGINX routes outside the catalog just to satisfy one publication request.

promote-to-production.description: Promote a staged service change into production only after the ADR 0073 validation, staging-evidence, and operator approval gates pass.

inputs.name: bootstrap_ssh_private_key
inputs.description: Controller-side SSH key used by the underlying production live apply.

inputs.name: branch
inputs.description: The codex/ or operator/ branch that produced the staged receipt being promoted.

inputs.name: staging_receipt
inputs.description: Repo-relative path to the staging live-apply receipt that proves recent staged verification.

expected_preconditions: make validate passes on the exact repo state being promoted
The referenced staging receipt exists under receipts/live-applies/staging and is less than 24 hours old
At least one human operator approval is recorded for the promotion request before production execution

evidence.notes: Record the staging receipt, the approved gate outcome, the production live-apply receipt, and the linked promotion receipt under receipts/promotions/.

stop_conditions: Stop if the staging receipt is stale, incomplete, or shows any non-pass verification result.
Stop if command-catalog approval is missing or if the observation loop reports open critical findings for the affected service.
rollback_guidance: Do not continue into production until a fresh staging apply and clean gate pass exist.
If production fails after an approved promotion starts, record the failed evidence and revert with the last known-good repo state through the normal live-apply path.

run-drift-detection.description: Run the ADR 0091 multi-source drift suite and record a fresh drift receipt before operators reconcile production drift.

inputs.name: bootstrap_ssh_private_key
inputs.description: Controller-side SSH key used by the remote build execution path and Docker-host inspection jumps.

inputs.name: proxmox_api_token_payload
inputs.description: Controller-local Proxmox API token payload required by the OpenTofu drift source.

inputs.name: nats_jetstream_admin_password
inputs.description: Controller-local password used when the drift run publishes internal NATS drift events.

inputs.name: ENV
inputs.description: Target environment, normally production.

expected_preconditions: make validate passes on the repo state being checked
The build server workspace is reachable through the ADR 0082 remote execution path
Any workstream that should suppress expected drift is already marked in_progress in workstreams.yaml before the run starts

evidence.notes: Record the generated drift receipt, the summary status, and any follow-up reconciliation branch or runbook used to resolve unsuppressed drift.

stop_conditions: Stop if the build server cannot run the drift suite or if any source fails before a receipt is written.
Stop if the resulting drift indicates an unexpected production incident that should be investigated before applying more changes.
rollback_guidance: Do not reconcile directly from shell history; return to the owning repo workflow or runbook for the affected source.
If the drift run itself was noisy because a workstream was missing suppression metadata, fix the workstream registry and rerun before escalating.

run-restore-verification.description: Run the ADR 0099 restore-verification workflow so PBS backups are restored into short-lived staging VMs and validated with both smoke tests and ADR 0190 synthetic transaction replay before the next disaster-recovery event forces the first real restore.

inputs.name: bootstrap_ssh_private_key
inputs.description: Controller-side SSH key used to reach the Proxmox host and the restored guests over the managed jump path.

inputs.name: proxmox_api_token_payload
inputs.description: Controller-local Proxmox API token payload used by the restore-verification workflow for VM teardown safety and the surrounding Proxmox automation context.

expected_preconditions: make validate passes on the repo state being used for the restore-verification run
The staging bridge exists on the Proxmox host and the controller-to-host jump path is healthy before any restore starts
Enough local storage capacity exists for the temporary restored VMs so the workflow can complete and destroy them cleanly

evidence.notes: Record the dated restore-verification receipt, the selected backup dates, any failed smoke tests, the synthetic replay success rate and latency summary, and the cleanup outcome for VMIDs 900 to 909.

stop_conditions: Stop if PBS backup listing fails, a restore cannot be created, or any temporary VM cannot be cleaned up after the run.
Stop if the staging bridge or SSH path is unhealthy enough that restore success cannot be distinguished from environment failure.
rollback_guidance: Destroy any leftover restore-verification VMIDs immediately before retrying; do not leave ambiguous restored guests running on the host.
Treat repeated restore failures as backup health incidents and investigate the selected PBS recovery points before changing unrelated platform automation.

run-backup-coverage-ledger.description: Run the ADR 0271 backup coverage ledger so governed VM backups and the backup-of-backup path are measured from live Proxmox evidence instead of assumed from static configuration.

inputs.name: bootstrap_ssh_private_key
inputs.description: Controller-side SSH key used to query the Proxmox host backup jobs and storage listings over the managed operations path.

expected_preconditions: make validate passes on the repo state being used for the backup-coverage run
The controller-to-Proxmox SSH path is healthy enough to read pvesh and pvesm output from the live host
The service redundancy catalog remains the governed declaration of which VM-level backup sources matter for production recovery

evidence.notes: Record the dated backup-coverage receipt, the governed asset summary, every uncovered asset, and any live repair commands that were required before the final rerun.

stop_conditions: Stop if the Proxmox host cannot be queried reliably enough to distinguish a real coverage gap from a connectivity failure.
Stop if the governed backup sources in the service redundancy catalog no longer map cleanly to the current VM inventory.
rollback_guidance: Treat uncovered assets as policy failures and repair the governed backup job or storage path before retrying.
If an ad hoc backup is required to verify a newly added governed VM, document that action in the live-apply receipt and keep the scheduled job as the durable path.

open-maintenance-window.description: Open one governed ADR 0080 maintenance window for a planned live change so expected alert noise is suppressed.

inputs.name: REASON
inputs.description: Planned change reason recorded inside the maintenance window payload.

inputs.name: DURATION_MINUTES
inputs.description: Expected window duration in minutes, capped operationally by the bucket max age.

expected_preconditions: make preflight WORKFLOW=open-maintenance-window passes
make validate passes before the window is opened
The affected service and planned downtime reason are already known before the live mutation begins

evidence.notes: Record the service id, reason, expected duration, and verification that the window is visible through the governed maintenance-window query surface.

rollback_guidance: Close the maintenance window immediately if the downstream live change is cancelled.
Do not proceed with the planned mutation until the suppression state matches the intended service scope.

close-maintenance-window.description: Close one governed ADR 0080 maintenance window when planned maintenance ends or is cancelled.

expected_preconditions: make preflight WORKFLOW=close-maintenance-window passes
make validate passes before the close is attempted
Operators confirm the planned change has finished, been cancelled, or needs a deliberate force-close

evidence.notes: Record the service id, whether force-close was used, and the resulting governed maintenance-window query output.

stop_conditions: Stop if the private NATS KV bucket cannot be reached or the targeted window cannot be removed.
Stop if the request would leave the intended service suppressed after the change window should be over.
rollback_guidance: Re-open the correct service-scoped maintenance window if an accidental close happens mid-change.
Prefer a scoped close over FORCE=true unless the active suppression state is already wrong.

rollback_guidance: Restore the last known-good roster on main and rerun the governed sync if an incorrect roster was merged.

token-exposure-response.description: Run the ADR 0141 exposure-response workflow for one governed token and record the resulting incident receipt.
